fields.Company Verticals: Chemistry & Materials, Advanced Manufacturing & Robotics, Energy Storage & Batteries
fields.Biography: Christopher Abram is the CTO of HiT Nano, a company developing advanced manufacturing for energy storage materials, including lithium- and sodium-ion battery cathodes. He grew up in Cornwall, U.K., and studied mechanical engineering at Bristol University and Imperial College London, focusing on thermal and fluid science, optical metrology, and particle technology. He has worked in Magdeburg, Germany, and Princeton, New Jersey, as a Marie Curie Fellow to develop aerosol technology for manufacturing luminescent particles and battery materials.

fields.Description: Discovering microbial ensembles that benefit people and plants
fields.Biography: Cheri Ackerman Araromi is co-founder and CEO of Concerto Biosciences. Previously, she was an NIH Kirschstein Postdoctoral Fellow at the Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard. Ackerman Araromi earned her Ph.D. in chemistry as a Hertz Fellow at UC Berkeley where she also served on the Chemistry Graduate Life Committee. She holds a B.S. in biochemistry and Spanish from Calvin University.

fields.Description: A new perspective on smart imaging
fields.Biography: Jesse Adams earned his Ph.D. in applied physics from Rice University where he designed and tested novel cameras using computational imaging for both photography and microscopy with applications ranging from three-dimensional imaging to recording neural activity to face detection. While at Rice University, he worked with Professor Ashok Veeraraghavan with whom he co-founded Synopic to continue developing ground-breaking, computationally driven imaging technology.

Alec Ajnsztajn is the founder and CEO of Coflux Purification. He received his B.S.E. from Duke University and a Ph.D. in materials science and nanoengineering from Rice University. Additionally, he co-founded the Fair for Houston/Yes on Prop B campaign in Houston, a citizen-led ballot initiative to reform regional infrastructure planning. He is passionate about how innovation can improve infrastructure and create a better world for all.

Mert Akin, founder and CEO of mining technology company EELI, holds a Ph.D. from the University of Miami with a focus on electrochemical energy storage. Formerly a scientist at a leading battery company, Akin led advancements in battery chemistries and recycling processes. Coming from the battery industry, Akin is on a mission to fortify the domestic battery supply chain by developing sustainable and efficient critical mineral extraction technologies leveraging innovations in electrochemistry and materials science.

fields.Description: Powering the next generation of the IoT
fields.Biography: Erkan Aktakka founded ActiveMEMS to commercialize his decade-long research on micro-electro-mechanical systems (MEMS) at the University of  Michigan, Ann Arbor, where he received his Ph.D. Aktakka received the University of Michigan Distinguished Achievement Award and DTE Clean Energy Prize and has served as a Technical Program Committee member at the international conferences of PowerMEMS and IEEE Sensors.

fields.Company Verticals: Advanced Manufacturing & Robotics, Chemistry & Materials, Energy Storage & Batteries
fields.Biography: Bilen Akuzum is the co-founder and CTO of Aepnus Technology. Previously, he was a postdoctoral fellow at the UC Berkeley Water Center. Akuzum's interdisciplinary academic path exposed him to a core foundation in metallurgy which expanded into elements of mechanical engineering, carbon materials, electrochemistry, and rheology as he pursued his Ph.D. at Drexel University. Akuzum is passionate about applying recent breakthroughs in electrochemical research to century-old problems in order to help decarbonize the manufacturing of critical materials.

fields.Description: Actionable climate intelligence for a resilient earth
fields.Biography: Adrian Albert, who died tragically on June 5, 2020, founded Terrafuse in 2018 to improve climate and weather risk prediction infrastructure. Before that, he was a machine learning research scientist at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory, designing machine learning algorithms incorporating physical knowledge for scientific applications. Albert earned his Ph.D. in electrical engineering at Stanford. At startup C3.ai, he worked on AI methods for energy and industrial systems.

Yasmeen Alfaraj is the CEO and founder of NextSet Materials, a company developing next-generation thermoset materials that enable the separation and recovery of desirable and expensive raw materials from thermoset composites. Hailing from a small coastal oasis in the Eastern Province of Saudi Arabia, Alfaraj completed her B.S. in chemistry at UC Berkeley, and her Ph.D. in chemistry with a specialization in polymers and soft matter at MIT.

Mo Alkhadra is the co-founder and CEO of Lithios, a startup developing disruptive technologies and processes for advanced lithium extraction. As a chemical engineer, Alkhadra is passionate about lowering current energy needs in the mining sector and catalyzing industrial electrification and decarbonization. He is also an environment enthusiast and hopes to leverage his experience to shape the future of minerals extraction and refining. Alkhadra earned a Ph.D. in chemical engineering from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology in 2022.

Now at Lumafield, Matt Alpert is leveraging his Activate journey to bring CT scanning to a new place in industrial manufacturing by making it cheaper, faster, and easier to use. In addition to his PhD in Chemical Engineering from the University of Virginia where he worked on quantum dots, next-generation perovskite solar cells, and novel scintillator materials, he has several years’ experience in large-scale industrial manufacturing as well as experience founding a VC-backed startup (RadiantNano).

Rodrigo Alvarez-Icaza is founder and CEO of Elysium Robotics, where he is replacing electric motors with muscle-like actuators to enable massive deployment of highly capable and low-cost robotic systems. He earned a Ph.D. from Stanford University in bioengineering (neuromorphic engineering) and was a key designer and member of the IBM team that built the first neural network accelerator, TrueNorth. Formerly a professor, Alvarez has more than 20 years of industrial and research experience.

Matthew G. Anderson is founder and CEO of Ọsọ Semiconductor, an innovative fabless semiconductor company selling ultralow-power, low-cost, performance microchips to supercharge the antennas of the future. Previously, he was an electrical engineering and computer science Ph.D. student at the University of California, Berkeley, specializing in low-power wireless microchip design. Anderson has more than six years of industry experience at Apple and other tech companies as well as a bachelor’s degree in electrical engineering and physics.

Hector Andrade is the co-founder and CEO of Lucidean. Andrade obtained his Ph.D. in electrical and computer engineering from UC Santa Barbara. Before this, he was employed at Intel's Silicon Photonics Products Division. Andrade's research centers on creating loss-tolerant fiberoptic interconnects to enable energy-efficient next-generation data centers and artificial intelligence and machine learning networks.

fields.Company Verticals: Food & Agriculture, Carbon Management / CO2e, Chemistry & Materials
fields.Biography: Wyatt Arnold, co-founder and CEO of Prospect Growth, grew up planting trees on his family's timber farm in Indiana. Experiences like this inspired a career devoted to the natural world. He holds a B.S. from the University of Chicago and a Ph.D. from Yale University. Supported by a National Defense Science and Engineering Graduate Fellowship, his doctoral work focused on developing methane-suppressing fertilizers. At Prospect Growth, he is advancing nanoengineered fertilizers to help farmers grow more with less.

fields.Company Verticals: Chemistry & Materials, Industrial Biotechnology, Food & Agriculture, Electronics & Connectivity
fields.Biography: Prior to founding Perceptra Technologies, Amir Atabaki was a research scientist at Research Laboratory of Electronics, MIT, where he worked on the integration of photonics and CMOS electronics for next-generation system-on-a-chip optical sensing and interconnect solutions. He received his Ph.D. from Georgia Tech, and his M.S. and B.S. in electrical engineering from Sharif University of Technology, Iran.

fields.Company Verticals: Climate, Chemistry & Materials, Energy Storage & Batteries
fields.Biography: Mariam Awara is co-founder and chief operating officer at Pulsenics. She trained as an electrical and computer engineer at the University of Calgary, where she researched power and energy systems and wireless telecommunication systems. Today she is on a mission to enable the adoption of clean energy processes at scale.

fields.Description: Affordable Grid Batteries from Iron and Salt
fields.Biography: Antonio Baclig studied batteries as a Ph.D. and then postdoc in Stanford’s materials science and engineering department, where he spearheaded the research of novel high-energy density electrolytes for flow batteries. Prior to that, he was one of the first employees at a venture-backed clean energy start-up (C12 Energy), working on a wide range of technical and business tasks. Baclig received his bachelor's degree from Harvard in chemistry and physics.

fields.Company Verticals: Climate, Chemistry & Materials, Energy Generation & Delivery
fields.Biography: Tandem PV co-founder Colin Bailie holds a B.S. in mechanical engineering from Texas A&M University and a M.S. and Ph.D. in materials science and engineering from Stanford University. He performed seminal research on perovskite tandem solar cells, including the first reports of mechanically-stacked perovskite on Silicon and CIGS tandems and monolithic perovskite on Silicon tandem. Bailie was included in the 2016 Forbes 30 under 30 in Energy list.

Harish Banda is a co-founder and CEO at Daqus Energy, a startup commercializing Li-ion batteries with organic cathode materials. He conducted his Ph.D. studies at the University of Grenoble Alpes, France, and worked as a postdoctoral researcher at MIT from 2019 to 2022. At MIT, he developed fast-charging organic electrode materials for energy storage in collaboration with Lamborghini, an Italian carmaker.

fields.Description: Plastic from CO2 and inedible biomass
fields.Company Verticals: Chemistry & Materials, Industrial Biotechnology, Carbon Management / CO2e
fields.Biography: Aanindeeta hails from India, where she studied chemistry as an undergraduate student. She came to the U.S. for Ph.D. studies at Stanford University and joined the lab of Matt Kanan. In her graduate work, she invented a new process for carbon dioxide utilization with applications in polymer and commodity chemical production. Upon completing her Ph.D. in 2018, she continued developing this technology with support from ARPA-E. In 2019, Banerjee and Professor Kanan founded ReSource

Cara Beasley is an expert in carbon materials, particularly single-walled carbon nanotube growth. As a process engineer at Applied Materials, she spent four years learning about the semiconductor industry and process tool development, while also continuing her work on new materials. Beasley holds a Ph.D. in physical chemistry from Stanford University and a B.S. in biochemistry from the University of California, Santa Barbara.

fields.Company Verticals: Climate, Chemistry & Materials, Carbon Management / CO2e
fields.Biography: Marissa Beatty recently completed her Ph.D. in the chemical engineering department of Columbia University, where her research focused on developing universal design principles for an emerging class of novel electrolyzer catalyst structures. She now applies these principles towards the design of robust electrolyzers that convert carbon dioxide into valuable chemical fuels, which can operate under harsh or impure conditions without sacrificing device efficiency, selectivity, or lifetime.

Ashley Beckwith is the founder and CEO of FORAY bioscience, where she is reimagining the production of tree-sourced goods and materials. Beckwith holds a Ph.D. and master’s degree in mechanical engineering from MIT. Her research and industry experience spans bioengineering, biomaterials, product design, and advanced manufacturing. Most recently, Beckwith has developed new methods for the net-shape production of customizable plant materials in vitro.

Behtash Behin-Aein received his Ph.D. in electrical and computer engineering from Purdue University in 2010. His spintronics research was one of the rare theoretical publications in Nature Nanotechnology. Later, at GLOBALFOUNDRIES, Behin-Aein led the development of device and compact models for STT-MRAM chips from concept to manufacturing 40Mb arrays. The decade-long pursuit of his passion, probabilistic computing, has led him to co-found a startup to actualize a new computing platform.

Eric Bellefroid is the founder and CEO of Brekland, an agricultural technology company building the tools to strengthen farmers’ resilience against climate change. After growing up on a 900-acre dairy and grain farm, Bellefroid completed a B.Sc. at McGill University and a Ph.D. in geochemistry at Yale University. Before starting Brekland, he worked at McKinsey & Company as an engagement manager focusing on business strategy and innovation in chemicals and agriculture.

fields.Description: Eliminating energy waste via molecular separation
fields.Company Verticals: Climate, Chemistry & Materials, Carbon Management / CO2e
fields.Biography: Francesco M. Benedetti is co-founder of Osmoses and a postdoctoral associate in chemical engineering at MIT. He earned his Ph.D. in chemical engineering from the oldest university in the world: University of Bologna, Italy. Benedetti received a 2019/2020 MIT Energy Fellowship and was an NSF I-Corps Entrepreneurial Lead in 2021. Benedetti and his team are committed to bringing membrane technologies from the lab to the global market, creating a sustainable future for energy.

fields.Company Verticals: Advanced Manufacturing & Robotics, Transportation & Mobility, Computing, Space & Aeronautics
fields.Biography: Justin Beroz is the founder and CEO of ReynKo Inc., which provides technical solutions for turbulent fluid flows. Beroz holds a Ph.D. in mechanical engineering and a Ph.D. in physics from MIT. He believes in an intimate connection between fundamentals and hands-on skills; in short, nothing is more practical than a good theory. Recently, Beroz penned a closed-form mathematical framework for turbulent fluids, constituting the foundation for tech development at ReynKo.

Jonathan Bessette is the founder and CEO of KIRA, a company working at the nexus of energy and water management. Bessette completed a B.S. at the SUNY University at Buffalo and a Ph.D. in mechanical engineering at MIT. Before starting KIRA, he worked on projects ranging from ice-penetrating radar to IoT sensors for monitoring rural water fetching. Bessette is passionate about bringing desalination and water treatment to previously unreached environments.

Raj Bhakta studied smart textiles in Jesse Jur's research group at the North Carolina State University's Wilson College of Textiles, where he earned a doctorate in fiber and polymer science. Bhakta earned a B.S. in physics from the University of Texas-Austin and sees textiles as a huge white-space opportunity for innovation.

David Bierman earned his Ph.D. in mechanical engineering from MIT, where he helped develop a novel solar-driven thermophotovoltaic converter capable of turning heat into electricity by absorbing thermal radiation using a photovoltaic cell. This innovation helped revitalize the field and was featured in a number of media outlets, including Scientific American and IEEE Spectrum. In 2018 he shuttered Marigold Power, to co-found Antora Energy with Andrew Ponec and Justin Briggs.

Monika Bleszynski is the founder and CEO of Greenshoot Materials, a sustainable packaging company developing compostable, non-toxic, cellulose-based coatings designed to replace plastic in food packaging. She holds a Ph.D. in materials science from the University of Denver, where she specialized in polymer materials. Bleszynski combines deep technical expertise with entrepreneurial drive to advance scalable, high-impact solutions to plastic pollution.

fields.Company Verticals: Carbon Management / CO2e, Chemistry & Materials, Food & Agriculture
fields.Biography: Brenna Boehman is a geochemist focused on carbon cycling and climate innovation. She recently served as Chief Science Officer at Sinkco Labs, where she led science and measurement, reporting, and verification development for a novel marine carbon storage approach. Her work builds on a Ph.D. from MIT and Woods Hole Oceanographic Institution, where she studied carbon export and preservation in terrestrial-marine systems. Boehman brings a deep commitment to scientific rigor, interdisciplinary collaboration, and equitable climate solutions.

fields.Description: Design and manufacturing of seismic metamaterials
fields.Company Verticals: Built Environment, Chemistry & Materials, Space & Aeronautics
fields.Biography: Noemi Bonessio is the inventor of a novel class of metamaterials with advanced vibration-control properties for the seismic protection of equipment, buildings and infrastructure. She has been an NSF I-corps entrepreneur lead, project scientist in the mechanical and aerospace engineering department at UC Irvine, postdoctoral scholar in the structural engineering department at UC San Diego, and research affiliate at the Caltrans SRMD testing facility. Bonessio holds a Ph.D. in structural engineering from the Sapienza University of Rome.

fields.Description: "Empowering the world with high-quality, high-impact carbon removal."
fields.Biography: Garrett Boudinot is the founder and CEO of Vycarb, an early-stage startup on a mission to empower the world with high-quality, high-impact carbon dioxide removal. Climate change has been the sole focus of Boudinot’s career, from carbon chemistry and ecosystem impacts to social adaptation and wildlife conservation. Boudinot earned his Ph.D. at the University of Colorado, holds dual bachelor's degrees in humanities and environmental geosciences from the College of Charleston, and served as a research associate at Cornell University. Boudinot also serves on the New York State Climate Impacts Assessment and the ClimateMusic Project.

fields.Company Verticals: Chemistry & Materials, Industrial Biotechnology, Life Science
fields.Biography: Christina Boville received her bachelor's degree in molecular biology from the University of Colorado. After working at Amgen for two years, Boville returned to the University of Colorado and earned her Ph.D. in biochemistry in Hubert Yin's group. She then joined Nobel laureate Frances Arnold's lab at Caltech as a Resnick Prize Postdoctoral Fellow. Her current research focuses on using directed evolution to train enzymes that make useful products in a sustainable way.

fields.Description: A new chip architecture for optimization problems
fields.Biography: Suraj Bramhavar is co-founder and CTO of Sync Computing. Previously, he held research positions at MIT Lincoln Laboratory and Intel Labs, where his research focused on integrated photonics for next-generation computing systems. He received his B.S., M.S., and Ph.D. in electrical engineering from Boston University.

Justin Briggs holds a B.S. in physics from UC Berkeley and a Ph.D. in applied physics from Stanford University. During his Ph.D., Justin developed next-generation renewable energy technology, with a focus on cost-effective climate solutions and solid-state energy conversion. His research studied the properties and performance of high-temperature materials for energy generation and storage applications. At Antora, Justin has played nearly every role required to build an energy storage company from the ground up.

Lance Brockway received a Ph.D. in chemical engineering from Texas A&M University in 2014. Following his doctorate work, he was a visiting scholar at the Jet Propulsion Laboratory and a postdoc at a joint venture between UC Berkeley and the National University of Singapore. There, he led R&D and manufacturing teams to reinvent the air conditioner for efficient operation in tropical climates.

fields.Company Verticals: Food & Agriculture, Chemistry & Materials, Energy Generation & Delivery
fields.Biography: Laron Burrows is the founder and CEO of Andros, a company commercializing low-pressure ammonia synthesis reactors for distributed ammonia production. Burrows earned a B.Sc. in chemical engineering from Kettering University and worked for DTE Energy as a reliability engineer for a 55-megawatt biomass power plant. As a Ph.D. student at the University of Connecticut, he invented Andros’ core technology and investigated distributed ammonia production for future use cases and alternative feedstocks.

fields.Company Verticals: Built Environment, Energy Generation & Delivery, Electronics & Connectivity
fields.Biography: Sam Calisch is co-founder of Channing Copper, a startup building home appliances engineered for the decarbonized future. He also leads special projects at the nonprofit Rewiring America, which advocates for widespread electrification. Previously, Calisch founded Elmworks, which is commercializing additive manufacturing of precision electromagnetics. He earned his Ph.D. from MIT’s Center for Bits and Atoms. Before MIT, he worked at Otherlab, building analytical and computational tools for advanced manufacturing and energy projects.

Xiangkun (Elvis) Cao is the co-founder and CTO of Arbon. He holds a Ph.D. in mechanical engineering from Cornell University. Cao received the prestigious Schmidt Science Fellowship to work on integrated carbon capture and utilization at MIT. He served as an Inaugural Impact Fellow at the MIT Climate & Sustainability Consortium and landed on Forbes 30 Under 30 list for his work on CO₂ use.

fields.Description: Carbon transformation for a climate positive world
fields.Company Verticals: Climate, Chemistry & Materials, Carbon Management / CO2e
fields.Biography: Etosha Cave is co-founder and Chief Scientific Officer of Twelve (formerly Opus 12) and an expert in the electrochemical conversion of carbon dioxide into fuels and high-value chemicals. She holds a Ph.D. in mechanical engineering from Stanford and has been an Accel Innovation Scholar, an Echoing Green Fellow, and a Smithsonian Institution Innovators to Watch. In 2018, Vanity Fair named Cave one of 26 Women of Color Diversifying Entrepreneurship in Silicon Valley, Media, and Beyond.

fields.Company Verticals: Chemistry & Materials, Advanced Manufacturing & Robotics, Carbon Management / CO2e, Earth Resources, Food & Agriculture, Industrial Biotechnology, Life Science
fields.Biography: Rakkiyappan Chandran is the former co-founder and CTO of Supercarb Inc., where he led pioneering efforts to convert carbohydrates from waste feedstocks into innovative biodegradable fibers. He holds a Ph.D. in nanoscience from the University of North Carolina at Greensboro and has extensive experience in textile and additive manufacturing. Chandran honed his expertise through research roles at the Wellman Center for Photomedicine at Massachusetts General Hospital and MIT.

Kezi Cheng is CEO of FLO Materials. She was recently named to the Forbes 30 Under 30 list in the manufacturing and industry category. Previously, she earned a Ph.D. from the Harvard John A. Paulson School of Engineering and Applied Sciences. She was an NSF Graduate Research Fellow, a Harvard Graduate Energy and Environment Consortium Fellow, and was on the organizing team for the inaugural Harvard Circular Economy Symposium. Cheng holds a bachelor's degree in materials science and engineering with a minor in management from MIT.

Charlie Childs is the co-founder and CEO of Intero Biosystems, where she is developing advanced stem-cell-derived human organoid models for safety testing. She holds a B.S. in biology from the University of Wisconsin–Madison and earned her Ph.D. in cell and developmental biology from the University of Michigan, where she also completed an embedded M.S. in computational medicine and bioinformatics.

fields.Company Verticals: Transportation & Mobility, Space & Aeronautics, Electronics & Connectivity
fields.Biography: Jae Cho is the CEO of Enertia Microsystems, which is commercializing low-cost, ultra-high-accuracy  micro-electro-mechanical systems (MEMS) inertial sensors. During his Ph.D. and postdoctoral training in electrical engineering at the University of Michigan, Cho co-invented a novel ultra-high precision MEMS gyroscope technology called the Birdbath Resonator Gyroscope (RBG). He co-founded Enertia Microsystems with his academic advisor, Khalil Najafi, to commercialize BRG technology.

fields.Description: A new chip architecture for optimization problems
fields.Biography: Jeffrey Chou is currently the VP of Machine Learning and AI at Capital One and the former co-founder and CEO of Sync Computing, which was acquired by Capital One in 2025. He received his Ph.D. in electrical engineering and computer science from UC Berkeley as an NDSEG fellow and was a Batelle postdoctoral scholar at MIT, with an emphasis on optical devices and materials for energy conversion. Most recently, Chou was a technical staff member at MIT Lincoln Laboratory with a research focus on novel computing paradigms, nanophotonics, and computational materials science.

Peter Christensen is the co-founder of FLO Materials. While he was a postdoctoral researcher at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory, Christensen invented the infinitely recyclable polymers known as polydiketoenamines (PDKs) that FLO is commercializing. He holds a Ph.D. in chemistry from the University of British Columbia. Christensen lives in San Diego with his wife and daughter and is currently exploring new projects.

Eugene Chung is the co-founder and CEO of Lift Biolabs, a biomanufacturing company developing low-cost, nanobubble-based purification reagents. He brings over a decade of experience in nanotechnology design across academic, military, and hospital-based research settings, with a focus on scalable process development. He earned his B.S. in microbiology from Arizona State University and is completing his Ph.D. in bioengineering at Rice University under the mentorship of Professor George Lu.

Victoria Coll Araoz is the co-founder and Chief Science Officer of SEMION, an agricultural technology company developing pest control strategies by restoring crops' natural defenses. With over 18 years of research experience, she holds a Ph.D. in phytochemistry from the National University of Tucumán in Argentina. Her work focuses on manipulating the chemistry mediating crop-pest-parasitoid interactions. A former Fulbright Scholar, she conducted research at the U.S. Department of Agriculture's Agricultural Research Service in Florida.

fields.Company Verticals: Electronics & Connectivity, Advanced Manufacturing & Robotics, Chemistry & Materials, Space & Aeronautics, Transportation & Mobility
fields.Biography: Rushin Contractor, co-founder and CTO of TopoLight is using his extensive experience in optoelectronics and photonic design to enhance the performance of semiconductor lasers. His expertise is built on research conducted during his Ph.D. in electrical engineering at UC Berkeley. Contractor also holds a B.Tech and M.S. in electrical engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology, Roorkee, and the University of Maryland, Baltimore County, respectively.

Andre Cornman is the co-founder and CTO of Tatta Bio. Cornman is a machine learning researcher with experience building deep learning models for genomics and robotics. Previously, Cornman was a senior software engineer at Waymo Research. Cornman earned a B.S. and M.S. in electrical engineering from Stanford University.

Gabriel Cossio is Founder and CEO of Nanoscale Labs. Previously, he was a postdoctoral fellow at the University of Texas’ Center for Dynamics and Control of Materials. Cossio earned his Ph.D. in electrical engineering at the University of Texas at Austin and has a B.S. in physics. Cossio performed seminal research in nanoparticle self-assembly, including the first demonstration of a low-cost and high-throughput nanomanufacturing system.

fields.Company Verticals: Carbon Management / CO2e, Climate, Chemistry & Materials, Life Science, Industrial Biotechnology
fields.Biography: Maeva Coste is the co‑founder and CEO of BioWraptor, a company that protects fragile biomolecules from heat, light, and oxidative degradation using peptide‑based microspheres. Originally from southern France, she earned a Ph.D. in chemistry and biomolecular engineering from the University of Montpellier, then pursued postdoctoral research at CUNY’s Advanced Science Research Center, where she co‑developed the technology behind BioWraptor. Before launching BioWraptor, Coste advised life‑science startups and participated in industry-academic innovation partnerships.

Taylor Cottle is the former co-founder of SHARP Diagnostics, a next-generation molecular diagnostic company that brings laboratory-level testing to the palm of your hand. Cottle completed a B.A. in biochemistry and music at the University of San Diego and a Ph.D. in biophysics at Johns Hopkins. He has received international recognition for his scientific, consulting, and entrepreneurial achievements. His passion is assembling great teams to translate research into tangible life science solutions for the people who need them most.

Menandro (Andre) Cruz is a founding scientist at Edulis Labs, where he develops safer, more sustainable hair dye technologies. Cruz holds a Ph.D. in chemistry from the University of Cambridge, where he focused on supramolecular polymer assemblies, and completed postdoctoral research at the University of Ottawa developing polymer dielectrics for organic electronics. He earned his bachelor’s degree in chemistry from the University of Toronto. His work combines polymer science, nanomaterials, and green chemistry to develop innovative, high-impact solutions.

Sonia Dagan received her doctorate in electrical engineering from the City College of New York. Before her doctorate, Dagan worked at Northrop Grumman, developing detector testing analyses for the Space-Based Infrared System surveillance payloads. Continuing with Northrop while pursuing a Ph.D., she built a neural network for background subtraction of clouds to detect thermal targets in satellite imaging. Dagan subsequently moved projects to design and built an eye-safe two-channel infrared lidar for aerosol and cloud detection.

Terence Davidovits is the founder and CEO of ThermoBionics. Previously, he held engineering and consulting roles in Boston-area energy and biotech companies. These roles focused on cooling system design for utility-scale battery storage, electric vehicles, bioreactors, and techno-economic consulting. Riding motorcycles first inspired him to create a wearable cooling system. He obtained his B.S. and M.Eng. degrees at Cornell University in mechanical and chemical engineering.

Ronald Davis III is the founder and CEO of VectorWave, a telecommunications startup developing AI hardware accelerators to enhance wireless communications in congested and challenging electromagnetic environments. Davis received his B.S., M.S., and Ph.D. from MIT, studying electrical engineering and physics. Prior to VectorWave, he worked for the U.S. Army Research Laboratory on spectrum control. He seeks to transition his research to transform spectrum access and wireless communications.

Nishita Deka holds a Ph.D. in electrical engineering from UC Berkeley and a B.S. in electrical engineering from the University of Southern California. She co-founded Sonera to commercialize a novel magnetic sensing technology for high-performance, non-invasive neural interfaces with the goal of enabling the next generation of computing, in which humans and technology are more intimately connected.

fields.Company Verticals: Climate, Built Environment, Chemistry & Materials, Earth Resources
fields.Biography: In 2021, Olivia Dippo earned her Ph.D. in materials science and engineering from UC San Diego, where she developed a new method for finding metal alloys optimized for 3D-printing, and invented new nitride and carbonitride ceramics with tunable optical and mechanical properties. Her expertise harnessing metallurgy, 3D printing, and materials' interactions with light inspires much of the technology that Limelight Steel, where she is co-founder and CEO, is developing.

Ryan DuChanois is the co-founder and CEO of Solidec, a Houston-based startup redefining chemical manufacturing. DuChanois has spent his career developing and deploying technologies around the globe. He previously completed a master’s degree in sustainability at Cambridge University, a Ph.D. in engineering at Yale University, and postdoctoral training at Rice University. He has published 20 peer-reviewed articles and received numerous awards, including the Gates Cambridge Scholarship and NSF Graduate Research Fellowship.

fields.Company Verticals: Chemistry & Materials, Industrial Biotechnology, Life Science, Carbon Management / CO2e
fields.Biography: Deepak Dugar is the founder of Visolis and brings deep expertise in bio-based products domain. Previously, he worked as a management consultant at PwC and consulted for DSM, NREL, Flagship Ventures and others. Dugar received the Legatum Fellowship and the Martin family society for sustainability fellowship. Dugar bachelor's and master's degrees in biochemical engineering and biotechnology from IIT Delhi and MBA, M.S. and Ph.D. in chemical engineering practice from MIT.

Sudharsan Dwaraknath is a social entrepreneur with over a decade of cross-disciplinary research experience. He received a Ph.D. in chemistry from the University of Illinois and completed postdoctoral work in synthetic biology at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory. Through conversations with smallholder farmers worldwide, he became committed to delivering solutions for vulnerable populations. Dwaraknath is building a company to harness agriculture as a force against climate change, while increasing sustainability and resilience to climate stress.

fields.Company Verticals: Industrial Biotechnology, Carbon Management / CO2e, Food & Agriculture, Transportation & Mobility
fields.Biography: Gabriella Dweck is a founder and co-CEO of Oleo, a venture transforming wasted biomass into sustainable oil. Before founding Oleo, Dweck worked as a process engineer at W. L. Gore & Associates in new product development and as a manufacturing engineer at Abbott Laboratories. She has an M.S. in engineering design impact from Stanford University and a B.S. in biomedical engineering from the Georgia Institute of Technology.

fields.Company Verticals: Climate, Chemistry & Materials, Energy Generation & Delivery
fields.Biography: Tandem PV co-founder and managing director Chris Eberspacher pioneered the development of efficient thin-film PV materials. He served as chief technology officer at SoloPower Systems, Hanwha, and at Applied Materials. Eberspacher earned a Ph.D. in applied physics from Stanford University and a BS in physics from the University of Texas.

fields.Company Verticals: Advanced Manufacturing & Robotics, Built Environment, Computing, Space & Aeronautics
fields.Biography: Nosa Edoimioya is the Founder and CEO of Reforge Robotics, a company that uses software and computation to improve the performance of robotic systems in manufacturing. He earned a B.S. in mechanical engineering from Stanford University and a Ph.D. in mechanical engineering from the University of Michigan, where he was a National Science Foundation research fellow. His mission is to make robots move as fluidly as humans, leading to symbiotic relationships between humans and robots.

fields.Company Verticals: Climate, Earth Resources, Energy Generation & Delivery, Computing
fields.Biography: Joel Edwards is a geophysicist with a background in subsurface imaging and geothermal exploration. Prior to co-founding Zanskar, he worked with Raser Technologies (now Cyrq Energy), BM Geothermal, and was a research assistant at the Great Basin Center for Geothermal Energy, and a graduate student with US Geothermal on the Neal Hot Springs geothermal field. Edwards earned a Ph.D. in earth sciences from UC Santa Cruz and an M.S. from the University of Nevada, Reno.

fields.Description: Crafting better seeds for a better future
fields.Biography: Prior to founding GigaCrop (formerly Perlumi), Chris Eiben was a postdoctoral researcher specializing in metabolic engineering and enzyme engineering. Eiben received a Ph.D. in bioengineering jointly awarded by UC Berkeley and UC San Francisco. He has many first-author publications in peer-reviewed journals, including in Nature Biotechnology and ACS Synthetic Biology. Eiben has also received several awards, including the prestigious NSF-GRFP fellowship.

fields.Description: An electrochemical pathway to cement production
fields.Biography: Leah Ellis is the CEO and co-founder of Sublime Systems, a company developing breakthrough technology to make low-carbon cement. Leah and co-founder, Dr. Yet-Ming Chiang, developed the technology while she was an NSERC/Banting Postdoctoral Fellow at the MIT Department of Materials Science and Engineering. Leah has a PhD in chemistry from Dalhousie University, was named among MIT Technology Review's 35 Innovators under 35, and is among the World Economic Forum's Technology Pioneers.

fields.Company Verticals: Climate, Chemistry & Materials, Energy Storage & Batteries
fields.Biography: Essam Elsahwi is co-founder and CEO at Pulsenics. Previously, he was the co-founder of an industrial electrochemical water treatment company and a hardware engineer at Advanced Micro Devices. He believes that advanced monitoring and control of clean processes are key to electrifying the manufacturing and transportation sectors. He holds a B.A.Sc. in engineering and economics and a Ph.D. in optimization of electrochemical systems from the University of Toronto.

Floris Engelhardt is CEO of Kano Therapeutics, which is expanding the reach of genome editing-based therapies. Previously, she was a postdoctoral researcher in the Bathe BioNanoLab at MIT. Engelhardt earned a Ph.D. in physics and a master's degree in molecular biotechnology from the Technical University of Munich. She holds a bachelor's degree in biochemistry and molecular biology from the Friedrich-Schiller University.

Luis Estevez is the founder and CEO of AIMM, a startup focused on clean water. His unorthodox career path began with obtaining a mechanical engineering degree at 35—after spending his twenties running his family’s Spanish restaurant. An internship at Los Alamos National Laboratory opened his eyes to a scientific career, inspiring him to obtain a Ph.D. in materials science at Cornell University, followed by a postdoctoral fellowship at Pacific Northwest National Laboratory, resulting in 40 peer-reviewed articles with over 5,000 citations.

fields.Company Verticals: Advanced Manufacturing & Robotics, Chemistry & Materials, Electronics & Connectivity
fields.Biography: Maya Faaborg is the founder and CEO of Capillary Nanotechnologies, which leverages manufacturing technology that she and a small group of other inventors developed at Harvard's School of Engineering and Applied Sciences to work on nanoscopic objects—work that cannot be done using other methods. Faaborg believes that the future of the technology is in manufacturing novel materials like nano-braids for next-generation electronics and medical devices.

fields.Description: Cleaner ways to make industry proven materials
fields.Biography: Cody Finke earned a Ph.D. in environmental science and engineering under Prof. Michael Hoffmann at Caltech. His research has specialized in electrochemistry and techno-economic modeling of economically efficient ways to reduce carbon dioxide emissions. Finke also helped commercialize an electrochemical wastewater treatment technology for applications in low-income countries.

Mael Flament is an expert in the design of precision scientific instrumentation. Prior to co-founding Qunnect, he conducted research at Stony Brook University, Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory, Brookhaven National Laboratory, CERN, and NASA. Qunnect’s product line is an extension of his graduate work developing a new generation of compact room-temperature quantum memories and quantum networking components in the Quantum Information Sciences & Technology laboratory at Stony Brook University.

Katherine French is the founder and CEO of BluumBio. She is passionate about developing new solutions to clean up the environment and bringing together multiple disciplines, from ecology to synthetic biology, to achieve this goal. She completed her bachelor’s at Boston University, her master’s and Ph.D. at the University of Oxford, as well as two postdocs at UC Berkeley and the Joint Bioenergy Institute.

Jessica Frick is the CEO of Astral Materials, building off her postdoctoral research in Professor Debbie Senesky’s XLab at Stanford University. She is passionate about using microgravity as a manufacturing tool to define humanity’s next Material Age. Frick earned her B.S. in chemistry from the University of Michigan and completed her Ph.D. in chemistry and materials science at Princeton University as an NSF Graduate Research Fellow, where she built her foundational knowledge on semiconductor crystal growth.

fields.Description: Advanced membranes for batteries and water treatment
fields.Company Verticals: Climate, Chemistry & Materials, Energy Storage & Batteries
fields.Biography: Peter Frischmann has devoted his career to advanced materials R&D. His background in self-assembly and hybrid organic/inorganic materials gives him a unique skill set for tackling long-standing energy storage problems. He was an Alexander von Humboldt Postdoctoral Fellow at the University of Würzburg and holds a Ph.D. in inorganic chemistry from the University of British Columbia and a B.Sc. in chemistry from Idaho State University.

fields.Company Verticals: Advanced Manufacturing & Robotics, Chemistry & Materials, Industrial Biotechnology
fields.Biography: Jill Fuss earned a B.A. in environmental science from Wesleyan University, and a Ph.D. in molecular and cell biology from UC Berkeley. She worked as a postdoctoral fellow and then a research scientist at Berkeley Lab. In 2012, she co-founded CinderBio with fellow Steve Yannone to commercialize extreme enzymes. After leaving CinderBio in 2021, she is now the managing director of the Activate Berkeley Community.

Alex Gagnon, co-founder and CEO of Banyu Carbon, earned his Ph.D. in chemistry from Caltech, a B.S. from UC Berkeley, and completed a postdoctoral fellowship at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory. Currently, on leave from the University of Washington faculty, he brings expertise in marine carbon, chemical oceanography, and climate to his science and business roles. Gagnon received a National Science Foundation CAREER Award.

Momcilo Gavrilov is CEO of SHARP Diagnostics, a biotechnology company developing rapid, portable, and affordable DNA amplification technologies. Gavrilov combines cutting-edge science, strategic partnerships, and top-tier talent to commercialize breakthrough innovations. He holds a B.Sc. in applied physics (University of Belgrade) and a Ph.D. in physics (Simon Fraser University) with a focus on thermodynamics of information. He completed a biophysics postdoc at Johns Hopkins University studying motor proteins. Previously, he developed diagnostics and sensing technologies at Honeywell International.

Joe Geddes founded Photia to enable rapid manufacturing of advanced materials for energy applications using holographic lithography. Prior to Photia, Geddes was an engineer at the company Rolith, where he made key contributions to the development of Rolling Mask Lithography, a method for making two-dimensional nanostructured coatings. He earned his Ph.D. in engineering science and mechanics from Penn State.

Pankaj Ghildiyal is the co-founder and CTO of 4th State Energies (fka SiLi-ion Inc), which develops plasma-created silicon to power next-generation lithium-ion batteries. Originally from the Indian Himalayas, he earned a Ph.D. in chemistry from the University of Maryland, focusing on gas-phase synthesis of nanostructured materials for energy applications. He now applies this expertise to advance high-performance, scalable battery materials. His work has been featured in Wired and The Wall Street Journal and supported by federal innovation grants.

Zach Gilvey received his master’s in mechanical engineering from Colorado State University, where he worked on a number of thermal fluidic projects focusing on improving efficiencies. As an environmentalist dedicated to achieving carbon neutrality, Gilvey co-founded Impact Cooling in order to save water and electricity for those who need them most.

fields.Company Verticals: Climate, Chemistry & Materials, Energy Generation & Delivery
fields.Biography: As the founder and CEO of Found Energy, Peter Godart is on a mission to address the climate crisis by turning waste materials into carbon-free, energy-dense replacements for fossil fuels. While working as a scientist at the NASA Jet Propulsion Laboratory, Godart developed spacecraft that could consume their vestigial aluminum components for energy. Now through Found Energy, he is building on his Ph.D. and postdoctoral work at MIT to help humanity do the same.

Nathan Good is the CTO of RareTerra, which he co-founded with N. Cecilia Martinez-Gomez, an assistant professor at UC Berkeley. Good is a microbial physiologist with a Ph.D. in microbiology from the University of Washington. As a project scientist and Bakar Innovation Fellow in the Martinez-Gomez lab at UC Berkeley, he developed a bio-platform for rare-earth element (REE) recovery and separation with support from ARPA-E. He ultimately aims to circularize the U.S. REE economy.

fields.Company Verticals: Advanced Manufacturing & Robotics, Built Environment, Chemistry & Materials, Computing
fields.Biography: Ian Goodine is the co-founder and CEO of rStream. Previously, he worked in various engineering roles at Glaukos. He received a B.S. and M.S. in mechanical engineering at the University of Massachusetts Amherst with research focuses in robotic biomimicry and polymer engineering. Goodine and his team are focused on creating robotic technology to solve the upstream challenges of waste and materials recovery.

fields.Company Verticals: Water, Electronics & Connectivity, Climate, Food & Agriculture, Chemistry & Materials
fields.Biography: Payton Goodrich is the co-founder and CTO of PAGE Technologies, a company providing an affordable agricultural and environmental data platform to optimize agricultural input use. Goodrich has had a lifelong interest in environmental conservation and enjoys technical challenges related to green technology development. Goodrich completed his B.Sc. at Iowa State University and a M.Sc. and Ph.D. in mechanical engineering at UC Berkeley.

fields.Company Verticals: Carbon Management / CO2e, Chemistry & Materials, Climate, Earth Resources
fields.Biography: Sumedh Gostu is the founder and CEO of FAST Metals, a company whose mission is to extract critical metals from industrial waste streams. Gostu holds an M.S. and Ph.D. in extractive metallurgy from Colorado School of Mines and Worcester Polytechnic Institute. Formerly employed in operational, research, and commercial roles in the mining industry, Gostu is on a mission to increase domestic production of critical metals from complex waste and feed sources, leveraging his experience with environmentally friendly hydrometallurgical extraction technologies.

Parker Gould co-founded InchFab in 2019 to commercialize low-cost fabrication tools and processes for "more-than-Moore" micro- and nanoscale devices and provide a new, flexible alternative to existing manufacturing options. He earned his Ph.D. from MIT's Microsystems Technology Laboratory, a M.Phil. in micro- and nanotechnology enterprise from the University of Cambridge, and a B.S. in electrical engineering from Vanderbilt University.

fields.Description: A new class of ultra-low-cost battery
fields.Biography: After receiving his Ph.D. from Columbia University, Chris Graves continued electrochemistry research at the Technical University of Denmark where he worked as a senior scientist. He founded Noon Energy in 2018 to commercialize a new type of low-cost long-duration battery he invented to enable 100% solar and wind power. Graves has led projects on novel electrochemical energy storage technologies, from new materials to modeling and characterization of cells to design and techno-economic analysis of systems.

Sam Green, former co-founder of Semiotic, has a strong foundation in applied math and embedded electronics. Leveraging these skills, he began his career in cryptographic hardware analysis, gaining expertise in statistics and machine learning. This experience paved the way for Green to pursue a Ph.D. at UC Santa Barbara, where he focused on developing AI agents for efficient reinforcement learning and decision-making under uncertainty.

fields.Company Verticals: Advanced Manufacturing & Robotics, Electronics & Connectivity, Computing
fields.Biography: Dan Green is the co-founder of PseudolithIC. Before that, he was a government executive at the Office of Naval Research and program manager at DARPA where he directed research advancing microelectronics. PseudolithIC’s semiconductor technology is derived from research into heterogeneous chip development that Green directed as part of the DARPA CHIPS program. Green received his Ph.D. from the University of California, Santa Barbara, and his B.S. from Yale.

Michael Grzenda earned his Ph.D. from Rutgers University in materials science after studying neuroscience at the University of Delaware. His graduate research focused on composite coatings, most recently using self-limiting electrospray deposition (SLED) to functionalize electrodes. He is working with his advisor, Jonathan Singer, to make SLED an advanced manufacturing platform. Notably, Grzenda aided in designing a coating for the first air/sea drone to reach 1,000 feet of ocean depth, collaborating with SubUAS LLC.

fields.Company Verticals: Advanced Manufacturing & Robotics, Chemistry & Materials, Energy Storage & Batteries
fields.Biography: Lukas Hackl is the co-founder and CEO of Aepnus Technology, a company focused on electrifying and decarbonizing the production of commodity chemicals. Aepnus' advanced electrolysis process evolved out of Hackl’s Ph.D. research on electrochemical water desalination that he carried out at UC Berkeley and Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory. Prior to that work, he received a master’s degree in mechanical engineering from ETH Zurich. Hackl is deeply passionate about climate change mitigation coupled with entrepreneurship.

fields.Description: Truly Compostable Plastic for a Circular Bioeconomy
fields.Biography: Aaron Hall is working to solve the plastic waste problem from the inside out. His diverse professional experiences in consumer packaged goods R&D, consulting, financial management, and strategy guide his work as founder of Intropic Materials. Hall earned his Ph.D. in materials science and engineering from UC Berkeley, where he led design and development of the novel enzyme stabilizing polymers at the core of Intropic, and a B.S. in chemistry from UC Santa Cruz.

Alexis Harroun is the founder and CEO of Juno Propulsion. She earned her Ph.D. from Purdue University, where she studied rocket propulsion, focusing her last six years of research on applying rotating detonation combustion to new rocket engine architectures. Harroun’s goal is to advance groundbreaking technologies to improve human and scientific access to space. She received her M.S. from Purdue and B.S. from the University of Washington.

Johnny Hergert is the co-founder and CTO of Manifest Technologies, an additive manufacturing company that provides tools to maximize manufacturers' flexibility to create never-before-possible objects. While obtaining his B.S. in chemistry from Oregon State, Hergert built his first 3D printer and has been studying and innovating in the additive industry for 10 years. He started Manifest Technologies with his co-founder while finishing a Ph.D. at the University of Colorado Boulder.

fields.Company Verticals: Climate, Built Environment, Carbon Management / CO2e
fields.Biography: Brendan Hermalyn is the founder and CEO of Thalo Labs. He is a scientist and engineer with a background in aerospace, environmental science, and sensing. Hermalyn has led and grown teams of tens to hundreds with annual operating budgets of up to $100 million. He holds a Ph.D. from Brown University and master’s degrees in engineering, mathematics, and geological sciences. His systems have been deployed on land, air, and space—from Antarctica to the moon. After appointments at the University of Hawaii and NASA Ames, he joined Skybox Imaging (acquired by Google) to run its space imaging systems group. Most recently, he led Waymo's camera program and GM/Cruise's autonomous vehicle hardware program.

fields.Company Verticals: Advanced Manufacturing & Robotics, Electronics & Connectivity, Computing
fields.Biography: Florian Herrault is the co-founder and chief executive officer of PseudolithIC. Previously he worked as a research scientist at the Georgia Institute of Technology and spent nine years at HRL Laboratories, developing technologies and leading programs and teams in the areas of radio frequency (RF) and mm-wave microelectronics.

Blake Herren is the CEO and Co-founder of Raven Space Systems. He became interested in space and 3D printing in high school and earned a B.S. in aerospace engineering. Herren started Raven while completing his Ph.D. in mechanical engineering at the University of Oklahoma, where he investigated advanced composites manufacturing, including 3D printing. He is passionate about making a large impact in the aerospace manufacturing industry using automated solutions.

Austin Hickman is Soctera's co-founder and CEO. He is working to develop millimeter-wave power amplifiers on the aluminum nitride platform for next-generation communication systems. In 2021, he earned his Ph.D. from Cornell University in electrical and computer engineering, and he holds a B.S. in the same field from the University of Notre Dame. Hickman is originally from Huntington, West Virginia, and coaches the Cornell Rowing Club.

fields.Description: CMOS RF Switches Facilitating Next-generation Wireless
fields.Biography: Cameron Hill's research interests include radio frequency and millimeter-wave integrated circuits, specifically in high linearity CMOS design methodologies, and their applications to reconfigurable radios. Prior to founding LintrinsIC he was a postdoctoral researcher at the University of California at Santa Barbara. His earned his Ph.D. in electrical engineering from UC Santa Barbara in 2020 and his M.E. and B.E. in electrical engineering from Stevens Institute of Technology in Hoboken, New Jersey.

David Hobby recently earned a Ph.D. in mechanical engineering from Colorado State University, where he developed a passion for sustainable cooling technologies while working on a variety of thermal systems. As co-founder of Impact Cooling, Hobby hopes to dramatically reduce the energy consumption and carbon footprint of data centers around the world through improved server cooling technologies.

Jason Hoffman-Bice is the co-founder and CEO of Fourier LLC, an advanced materials manufacturing company focused on multifunctional substrates, encapsulants, and enclosures for electronic systems. The inventor of Fourier's foundational technology, Thermoformable Ceramic materials and manufacturing process, Bice co-founded Fourier with his Ph.D. advisor, Randy Erb, in 2022. Bice completed his Ph.D. in mechanical engineering at Northeastern University, his M.S. in materials engineering at Purdue University, and his B.S. in mechanical engineering at the University of Florida. Prior to his academic career, he served in the U.S. Army.

fields.Company Verticals: Climate, Earth Resources, Energy Generation & Delivery, Computing
fields.Biography: Zanskar co-founder Carl Hoiland is an exploration geologist with experience researching the effects and timescales of plate tectonic interactions, including how those interactions control natural resource distributions. Previously, he performed hydrocarbon exploration with Shell and collaborated on the structural-stratigraphic framework of copper deposits in northern Alaska with Trilogy Metals. He holds a Ph.D. from the Department of Geological Sciences at Stanford University where he was an NSF Graduate Research Fellow.

fields.Company Verticals: Industrial Biotechnology, Carbon Management / CO2e, Chemistry & Materials
fields.Biography: Advait Holkar is the founder and CEO of Praio, a biomanufacturing startup focusing on low-cost production of biocommodities. He completed his bachelor's degree at IIT Delhi and obtained a Ph.D. in chemical engineering from UCLA. Holkar formed Praio based on his graduate research and is dedicated to reducing reliance on fossil-based sources for a sustainable and green future.

fields.Company Verticals: Food & Agriculture, Carbon Management / CO2e, Life Science
fields.Biography: Eli Hornstein is a conservation biologist turned genetic engineer and the founder of Elysia, a startup engineering plants to combat methane emissions. Hornstein previously worked on sustainability biotech in plant microbiomes, biofuels, and alternative proteins and has spent years living around the world as a conservationist. Hornstein has a Ph.D. in plant biology from North Carolina State University and is an alum of Duke University, University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill, and Harvard Business School. He has been awarded numerous fellowships, including from the Smithsonian Institution, Fulbright, the National Science Foundation, and the Robertson Foundation.

fields.Company Verticals: Climate, Chemistry & Materials, Energy Storage & Batteries, Electronics & Connectivity
fields.Biography: Andrew Hsieh is an expert in electrochemistry, materials science, and characterization. During his Ph.D., he developed novel anodes for Li-ion batteries. As a postdoc, Hsieh led two ARPA-E funded projects and co-invented Liminal's ultrasonic battery analysis technology. Hsieh holds a Ph.D. in chemical and materials engineering from Princeton University and a B.S. in chemical and biomolecular engineering from UCLA.

Mitchell Hsing co-founded Inchfab in 2019 to commercialize low-cost fabrication tools and processes for "more-than-Moore" micro- and nanoscale devices and provide a new, flexible alternative to existing manufacturing options. He earned a Ph.D. from Microsystems Technology Laboratory at MIT and his undergraduate degrees in electrical engineering and physics from UC Irvine.

fields.Company Verticals: Climate, Chemistry & Materials, Energy Storage & Batteries
fields.Biography: Wenxiao Huang is the CEO and co-founder of Feon Energy, which aims to develop batteries that permit electric vehicles to travel 700 miles on a 10-minute charge. Previously, he led development at EnerVenue, focusing on megawatt-level batteries using innovative chemistry, and was a postdoctoral research fellow at Stanford University, specializing in safety and fast charging for high-energy batteries. With a Ph.D. in physics, Huang has published over 40 articles and holds five patents.

fields.Company Verticals: Climate, Computing, Space & Aeronautics, Carbon Management / CO2e
fields.Biography: Claudia Hulbert is the co-founder and CEO of Geolabe, which is developing data science algorithms to tackle climate change issues. Geolabe has received support from NASA, DOE, and NSF small business innovation research programs. She earned her doctorate in machine learning and geoscience from the École Normale Supérieure in France. Before founding Geolabe, Hulbert worked in economic policy at the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development and at the Los Alamos National Laboratory.

Yunha Hwang is the co-founder and CEO of Tatta Bio, a scientific nonprofit building AI models, tools, and datasets to accelerate biotechnology research and development. She is passionate about unlocking the potential of uncultivated microbes for sustainable biosolutions. Hwang is a computational biologist by training, with a B.S. in computer science from Stanford University and a Ph.D. in biology from Harvard University. Previously, she was the head of data at a fungal biotechnology startup.

Brent Ifemembi is the co-founder and CEO of Drūl, a saliva diagnostic company building tools to diagnose chronic diseases. Ifemembi received his B.S. in chemical and biomedical engineering and M.S. in biomedical engineering from Carnegie Mellon and a Ph.D. in chemical and biomolecular engineering from Johns Hopkins University. Ifemembi has a passion for improving clinical outcomes, and his doctoral research focused on applying microfluidic technology to cancer prognostics, aiming to improve disease prediction and management.

Poolad Imany is an expert in quantum photonics and quantum networking with 11 years of experience. He received his Ph.D. in electrical and computer engineering from Purdue University, researching chip-scale quantum optics. Since then, he has been a postdoctoral associate at the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST), building efficient and scalable quantum light sources. At NIST, he founded Icarus Quantum to commercialize NIST’s quantum dot technology and enable scalable quantum networking.

fields.Company Verticals: Climate, Energy Generation & Delivery, Computing, Electronics & Connectivity
fields.Biography: Palak Jain is the co-founder and CEO of Vellex Computing. As a senior research associate at Cal Poly, San Luis Obispo, she conducted foundational research in computing and grid technologies that led to the formation of Vellex Computing. She received her Ph.D. in electrical and computer engineering from the National University of Singapore, where she was part of the Berkeley Education Alliance for Research in Singapore program.

Before co-founding SkyGig with Avish Kosari, Armin Jam was a research fellow at the University of Michigan, where he earned his Ph.D. and M.Sc. in electrical engineering. He specializes in millimeter-wave antennas and fabrication; RF integration and packaging; as well as high-frequency instrumentation. He has demonstrated the most power-efficient beamforming RF front-end reported at sub-millimeter-wave frequencies and has been awarded in several international venues.

Jiya Janowitz is the CTO of Astral Materials, where he is the team’s critical link between its materials manufacturing goals and building hardware that integrates with commercial space infrastructure. Janowitz completed his B.S. in materials science and his M.S. in aerospace engineering at Stanford University, where he performed research in Professor Debbie Senesky’s XLab. Before joining Astral Materials, Janowitz led the development of several successful automated science payloads that flew to the International Space Station.

fields.Description: Unlocking Metamaterials at the Macro Scale
fields.Biography: Ben Jenett completed his Ph.D. at MIT’s Center for Bits and Atoms, where the NASA Space Technology Research Fellowship supported his research. There, he developed discrete mechanical metamaterials, as well as robotic methods for their construction, and demonstrated their real-world applications in transportation, aviation, and aerospace. He has industry background in large scale digital fabrication and advanced structural engineering, and holds a B.A. from UC Berkeley and an M.S. from MIT.

Steven Jepeal is the CEO and co-founder of Allium Engineering. He began working on Allium while completing his Ph.D. in metallurgy in MIT's Nuclear Science and Engineering Department. Trained as a technical communicator in MIT's Communication Lab, he has spent 200 hours coaching his peers on effective communication. He is drawn to the biggest, toughest problems that technology can solve, which led him to found Allium.

Katy Jinkins is the co-founder and CEO of SixLine Semiconductor, a startup on a mission to finally enable next-generation, high-performance electronics based on carbon nanotubes. Jinkins co-invented the technology at the heart of SixLine through her doctoral work in Michael Arnold’s laboratory at the University of Wisconsin-Madison, where she earned her Ph.D. Before co-founding SixLine, Jinkins was a postdoctoral scholar in John Rogers’ lab at Northwestern University.

Yasamin Jodat is a bioengineer specializing in automation, synthetic biology, and high-throughput discovery. Her Ph.D. research at Harvard Medical School explored biosensing, engineered tissues, and 3D-printed biomimetics to unlock new biological models. With five years of industry experience building multi-million-dollar genomic engineering systems at Ginkgo Bioworks, Jodat now focuses on creating tools to decode complex molecular interactions, including the mysteries of the biology of smell.

Nadia Jorgenson is the co-founder and CTO of OsmoPure Technologies, which develops pressure-driven distillation membranes for spaceflight, ultrapure water, and wastewater reuse applications. Raised in the Middle East and North Africa, Jorgenson’s passion for water reuse began early. She earned her Ph.D. in environmental engineering at the University of Colorado Boulder, where she researched advanced treatment systems and contaminant control for potable reuse. She specializes in the development and integration of next-generation water treatment technologies.

Joey Kabel earned a Ph.D. in nuclear engineering, focused on experimental test development for high temperature ceramics from UC Berkeley. As a material scientist, he is passionate about applying the fundamentals of "cook and look" and "heat and beat" to solve our world's most pressing challenges. As a climate enthusiast, he hopes to leverage his experiences to shape the future of energy consumption.

fields.Description: Vitrimer matrix composites for the circular economy
fields.Biography: Chris Kaffer co-founded Mallinda in 2014 and served as its CEO until 2022. He led the company's funding and commercialization strategies scaling production volumes to the metric ton for delivery to OEMs and tier-1s. He is currently the Program Director for Innovyz USA; founding deep tech companies and providing a full capital solution to build organically sustainable businesses.

Jan Kaiser is a co-founder of Ludwig Computing. He earned his Ph.D. in electrical and computer engineering as a Ross fellow in Supriyo Datta’s lab at Purdue University. His research focused on spintronic devices and probabilistic computing systems. He is passionate about novel physics-inspired computing approaches. Kaiser earned bachelor's and master’s degrees in electrical engineering from Ruhr-University Bochum in Germany. Kaiser left Ludwig Computing in July 2023 and is now a software engineer at Lattice Semiconductor.

Fatma Kaplan is the CEO and chief science officer of Pheronym. While earning a Ph.D. in plant molecular and cellular biology, she identified the role of beta-amylase and maltose during cold and heat shock. She discovered the first Caenorhabditis elegans sex pheromone and found that pheromones regulate other behaviors in both parasitic and beneficial nematodes. She conducted the first agricultural biocontrol experiment in space at the International Space Station in 2020.

fields.Description: Advanced materials for a cleaner future
fields.Biography: Steven Kaye is an experienced leader in the areas of battery technology and R&D. He was part of Activate's 2015 cohort as co-founder, with fellow Thomas McDonald, of Mosaic Materials. He left Mosaic to lead materials R&D for batteries as part of Apple's special projects group. He is now the chief technology officer at ONE. Kaye earned his Ph.D. in chemistry from UC Berkeley.

fields.Description: Discovering microbial ensembles that benefit people and plants
fields.Biography: Jared Kehe is co-founder and CSO of Concerto Biosciences. Previously, he was an NSF Graduate Research Fellow and Siebel Scholar at MIT, where he completed his Ph.D. in biological engineering. He also served as president of the MIT Microbiome Club, copy co-chief of the MIT Grad Blog Editorial Board and was a Broad Institute Communication Lab Fellow. He earned undergraduate degrees in bioengineering and music composition from UC San Diego.

fields.Company Verticals: Industrial Biotechnology, Chemistry & Materials, Computing, Life Science
fields.Biography: Nikita Khlystov is co-founder and CEO of Huminly, the startup enabling infinite recycling of textile waste using enzymes. Growing up in low-income housing, Khlystov’s journey as an immigrant inspired him to work at the intersection of synthetic biology and sustainability. During college at MIT, he engineered bacteria to make bio-based plastics, and he studied lignin-degrading enzymes during his Ph.D. at Stanford University. He was awarded NSF and Stanford Graduate Fellowships and was named an Accel Innovation Scholar.

As co-founder and CTO of Lagomics, Kia Kiaee is pioneering multiplexed mapping of biomolecular interactions via DNA barcodes and next-generation sequencing. Kiaee earned a Ph.D. in bioengineering and has over five years of industry experience at Ginkgo Bioworks, GC Therapeutics, and Harvard Genetics (Church Lab), where he advanced genome editing and cell therapy platforms, including scalable iPSC workflows. Kiaee co-led a $9.4M ARPA-H award and designed scalable, cloud-based wet-lab and informatics pipelines to accelerate target discovery.

fields.Company Verticals: Climate, Built Environment, Carbon Management / CO2e
fields.Biography: Ioana Knopf is the founder and CEO of Arrakis Materials, a startup converting CO₂ into valuable minerals. Originally from Romania, Knopf relocated to the United States to pursue her interests in chemistry and environmental science and earned her Ph.D. in CO₂ utilization from MIT.  After spending five years in early-stage startups, transitioning technologies from bench-scale research and development to full-scale production, Knopf started Arrakis Materials with the mission of turning CO₂ into a useful resource.

fields.Company Verticals: Space & Aeronautics, Advanced Manufacturing & Robotics, Earth Resources, Food & Agriculture, Water
fields.Biography: Scarlett Koller is co-founder and CEO of Mithril, a space technology company building dynamic reflectors for a new era of flexible satellite communications and remote sensing. Koller completed a B.Sc. at MIT in aerospace engineering before entering the space industry. After five years with SpaceX and NASA Jet Propulsion Laboratory as an integration and test engineer, she returned to MIT for an M.S. in aerospace and an MBA at MIT Sloan. She and Professor Zack Cordero formed Mithril Technologies to realize the potential of the actuation technology being developed in his lab.

Before co-founding SkyGig with Armin Jam, Avish Kosari earned her Ph.D. and M.Sc. in electrical engineering from the University of Michigan, where she performed postdoctoral research on advanced millimeter-wave wireless technologies. She specializes in low-power mixed-signal and RF integrated circuits as well as high-performance wireless radio systems and architectures. Her research focuses on the gap between power consumption and performance in wireless systems and has resulted in new and record-performance transceiver wireless architectures.

Pavan Kota is the founder of Anvil Diagnostics. He earned his Ph.D. in bioengineering at Rice University in 2022, where he developed Anvil’s underlying technology and a belief that data-driven science and applications will increasingly rely on new paradigms for scalable sensing. Previously, Kota earned a B.S.E. in biomedical engineering from Case Western Reserve University.

fields.Description: Carbon transformation for a climate positive world
fields.Company Verticals: Climate, Chemistry & Materials, Carbon Management / CO2e
fields.Biography: Kendra Kuhl is co-founder and chief technology officer at Twelve (formerly Opus 12). She and Cohort 2015 fellow Etosha Cave launched Twelve after earning Ph.D.s in the Jaramillo Lab at Stanford University. Kuhl was named to MIT Technology Review's Innovators Under 35 list and The New York Times named her a visionary leader addressing climate change. Kuhl earned a B.S. from the University of Montana, both in chemistry.

Albert Kumar is the founder and CEO of Carbide Radio, a semiconductor company building novel silicon carbide radio frequency microchips with unprecedented performance. Prior to Carbide Radio, Kumar held various semiconductor industry roles including technology development, chip design, and sales at companies like Qualcomm and Xfab. He has a B.S. in electrical engineering from UCLA.

fields.Description: Harvesting value from agricultural & forest waste
fields.Company Verticals: Climate, Food & Agriculture, Carbon Management / CO2e
fields.Biography: Takachar chief technology officer Kevin Kung earned his Ph.D. from MIT, focusing on biofuels and renewable energy, specializing in biomass and energy conversion systems. In 2012, he co-founded Safi Organics, which turns agricultural residues into carbon-negative fertilizer. He then co-founded Takachar to further improve biomass conversion technology. Kung received his A.B. in physics from Princeton University, M.S. in physics from the University of Cambridge, and S.M. in biological engineering from MIT.

GJ la O’ is co-founder and chief executive officer of Tyfast. Before Tyfast, he spent a decade leading technology development and commercialization of grid-scale energy storage at Primus Power, where he helped raise $100M in venture capital. At Tyfast, la O' leads product, market, and funding roadmaps. He has published over 20 articles and has been issued 23 patents. He is a materials scientist and earned his bachelor’s degree from UC Berkeley and his Ph.D. from MIT.

Dominic Labanowski earned a Ph.D. in electrical engineering and computer sciences from UC Berkeley and a B.S. in electrical and computer engineering from The Ohio State University. He co-founded Sonera with Nishita Deka, whom he met while conducting graduate research, to optimize and develop resonant magneto-acoustic devices with diverse applications including magnetic sensing, antenna miniaturization, and control of quantum systems.

fields.Description: Eliminating energy waste via molecular separation
fields.Company Verticals: Climate, Chemistry & Materials, Carbon Management / CO2e
fields.Biography: Holden Lai is co-founder and CTO of Osmoses. He earned a B.Mus. in percussion performance and a B.A. in chemistry and materials physics from Oberlin College. Lai earned his Ph.D. in chemistry at Stanford University as an NSF Graduate Research Fellow in the Yan Xia lab, where he developed membrane materials with unprecedented performance for industrial gas separations. At Osmoses, he leads the materials research and development efforts.

Vernon LaLone is the founder and CEO of Wave Lumina, where he is developing portable field technology for real-time PFAS detection. LaLone has over a decade of experience in analytical chemistry and Raman spectroscopy across the pharmaceutical, biochemical, and materials science sectors. Prior to Wave Lumina, LaLone was a Marie Curie Postdoctoral Fellow at Imperial College London and led research and development programs in industry, overseeing multidisciplinary teams focused on advanced analytical method development.

fields.Company Verticals: Climate, Food & Agriculture, Energy Storage & Batteries
fields.Biography: Jose LaSalle is the co-founder and CEO of florrent. Before that, he worked as product manager and lead engineer at Ventacity Systems. He earned his B.S. in electrical engineering from the University of Massachusetts Amherst with a research focus on electrochemistry. LaSalle's goal is to commercialize solutions that contribute to the well-being of people and the planet, and he envisions his work supporting the transition to a renewable, resilient, and equitable energy economy.

fields.Company Verticals: Climate, Earth Resources, Energy Generation & Delivery
fields.Biography: Fervo Energy CEO Tim Latimer began his career as a drilling engineer with BHP Billiton. Motivated to confront climate change, he left the oil and gas industry in 2015 to pursue an M.B.A. and an M.S. in environment and resources from Stanford University. He holds a degree in mechanical engineering from the University of Tulsa.

fields.Description: Turning food waste into biobased products
fields.Company Verticals: Chemistry & Materials, Industrial Biotechnology, Food & Agriculture
fields.Biography: Luke Latimer is co-founder and CTO of ZestBio. He has led technology, scaling, and product development efforts at Zestbio including NSF and USDA SBIR awards. Prior to founding ZestBio in 2017, Latimer earned his Ph.D. in chemistry from UC Berkeley.

Nikita Lazarev, the co-founder and CTO of Netpreme, completed his Ph.D. in electrical and computer engineering at MIT, specializing in hardware design for emerging networking infrastructures and data center systems. Prior to grad school, Lazarev worked at Google and Microsoft as a visiting researcher on data center systems. Lazarev also holds a master's degree in computer science from EPFL in Switzerland and an undergraduate degree in electrical engineering from Bauman Moscow State Technical University.

fields.Description: "Digital Imaging Radar for Safe, Autonomous Mobility"
fields.Company Verticals: Advanced Manufacturing & Robotics, Transportation & Mobility, Electronics & Connectivity
fields.Biography: Jungah Lee is a co-founder and CEO of Aura Intelligent Systems, a company disrupting wireless systems with patented 4D sensing technology. She previously led advanced technology for 4G/5G networks at Samsung Networks and Altiostar and worked at Lucent Technologies-Bell Labs. She holds a B.S. and M.S. degrees in electronics engineering from Seoul National University, and a Ph.D. in electrical and computer engineering from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign.

Junho Lee is the co-founder and CEO of Deep Anchor Solutions, a startup developing innovative anchoring systems for floating renewables and offshore infrastructure. Lee holds a Ph.D. in geotechnical engineering from Texas A&M University and has over 15 years of experience in offshore foundation design and anchoring technologies. Before founding Deep Anchor Solutions, he led R&D and commercialization efforts across academia and industry to advance offshore energy infrastructure.

Marcus Lehmann is CEO and co-founder of CalWave Power Technologies, which is on a mission to unlock the vast and steady carbon-free power from ocean waves worldwide. CalWave was spun out of UC Berkeley's Theoretical & Applied Fluid Dynamics Laboratory, where Lehmann was a visiting scholar.
